Infra_water_electricity_status: mixed: direct ESPAC infrastructure + literature proxy water/electricity
Synthetic_headcount_basis_note: Headcount per kg is based on current inventory mass basis for live weight equivalent products.
Synthetic_feed_mapping_note: pasture total = ESPAC pasture + proxy forage + proxy tree forage; cultivated and natural pasture totals show only the ESPAC-linked split of cattle pasture, while unmatched pasture is the residual cattle pasture share not linked to pcnac or sunac; supplement = direct ESPAC per-kg supplement/proxy-mineral feed when available, otherwise ESPAC supplement + proxy mineral from head-day scaling; common = direct ESPAC per-kg common/proxy-compound feed when available, otherwise ESPAC common + proxy compound from head-day scaling; waste = direct ESPAC per-kg waste feed when available, otherwise ESPAC waste from head-day scaling
Infra_water_electricity_note: infrastructure from ESPAC cattle milking-system field; water/electricity from literature proxy
Infra_water_electricity_citations: FAO small-scale dairy manual - Uses 4 to 6 L water per kg dry matter intake; the worked example for a 300 kg cow producing 10 kg milk/day gives about 66 L/day, adopted here as the dairy drinking-water proxy. (https://www.fao.org/4/t1265e/t1275e01.htm) | FAO Livestock housing water requirements - Reports dry dairy cow water requirements around 40 to 60 L/day, supporting the order of magnitude adopted for low-intensity tropical dairy systems. (https://www.fao.org/3/S1250E/S1250E12.htm) | Uruguay MGAP dairy intermediate-consumption report - Provides Latin American dairy infrastructure energy benchmarks for milking-room electricity and milk cooling; a rounded 1.5 kWh/cow/day proxy was adopted as a moderate regional milking-plus-cooling estimate. (https://www.gub.uy/ministerio-ganaderia-agricultura-pesca/sites/ministerio-ganaderia-agricultura-pesca/files/documentos/publicaciones/informe_consumo_intermedio.pdf)

Infrastructure_type: donkey holding with shelter and trough
Synthetic_headcount_basis_note: Headcount per kg is based on current inventory mass basis for live weight equivalent products.
Synthetic_feed_mapping_note: pasture total = ESPAC pasture + proxy forage + proxy tree forage; cultivated and natural pasture totals show only the ESPAC-linked split of cattle pasture, while unmatched pasture is the residual cattle pasture share not linked to pcnac or sunac; supplement = direct ESPAC per-kg supplement/proxy-mineral feed when available, otherwise ESPAC supplement + proxy mineral from head-day scaling; common = direct ESPAC per-kg common/proxy-compound feed when available, otherwise ESPAC common + proxy compound from head-day scaling; waste = direct ESPAC per-kg waste feed when available, otherwise ESPAC waste from head-day scaling
Infra_water_electricity_note: ESPAC livestock modules do not expose direct infrastructure/water/electricity variables for this row; literature/system proxy attached
Infra_water_electricity_citations: WSU horse winter water note - Donkey water proxy was scaled from the horse baseline by representative body weight because no ESPAC or Ecuador-specific donkey water field was available. (https://s3.wp.wsu.edu/uploads/sites/2070/2024/01/1.24.pdf)

Infrastructure_type: non-cage layer house on deep litter with drinkers and lighting
Synthetic_headcount_basis_note: Egg headcount per kg is based on one laying cycle of 54 weeks and 300 eggs/hen/year, converted with 0.056699 kg/egg.
Synthetic_feed_mapping_note: pasture total = ESPAC pasture + proxy forage + proxy tree forage; cultivated and natural pasture totals show only the ESPAC-linked split of cattle pasture, while unmatched pasture is the residual cattle pasture share not linked to pcnac or sunac; supplement = direct ESPAC per-kg supplement/proxy-mineral feed when available, otherwise ESPAC supplement + proxy mineral from head-day scaling; common = direct ESPAC per-kg common/proxy-compound feed when available, otherwise ESPAC common + proxy compound from head-day scaling; waste = direct ESPAC per-kg waste feed when available, otherwise ESPAC waste from head-day scaling
Infra_water_electricity_note: ESPAC livestock modules do not expose direct infrastructure/water/electricity variables for this row; literature/system proxy attached
Infra_water_electricity_citations: Producción sostenible en avicultura - Reports 222.03 L per 1000 hens per day, equivalent to 0.222 L/hen/day; used as the preferred Latin American-style layer water proxy. (https://dokumen.pub/produccion-sostenible-en-avicultura-l-6359269.html) | FAO small-scale poultry production - Reports that a laying hen can consume up to about 0.25 L/day of water, supporting the adopted 0.222 L/hen/day proxy. (https://www.fao.org/4/y4628e/y4628e03.htm) | Iowa layer-house electricity summary - Layer-house electricity around 10.1 kWh/day per 1000 birds was used, equivalent to 0.0101 kWh/bird/day. (https://blogs.millersville.edu/aest/2022/05/13/dr-haughery-publishes-research-findings/) | FAO small-scale poultry production - Intensive deep-litter housing is described for egg production with about 3 to 4 birds per m2; the midpoint 3.5 birds per m2 was retained, equivalent to 0.286 m2 per hen, as the non-cage layer-house footprint proxy. (https://www.fao.org/4/y5169e/y5169e02.htm)

Infrastructure_type: broiler house or coop for meat birds
Synthetic_headcount_basis_note: Headcount per kg is based on current inventory mass basis for live weight equivalent products.
Synthetic_feed_mapping_note: pasture total = ESPAC pasture + proxy forage + proxy tree forage; cultivated and natural pasture totals show only the ESPAC-linked split of cattle pasture, while unmatched pasture is the residual cattle pasture share not linked to pcnac or sunac; supplement = direct ESPAC per-kg supplement/proxy-mineral feed when available, otherwise ESPAC supplement + proxy mineral from head-day scaling; common = direct ESPAC per-kg common/proxy-compound feed when available, otherwise ESPAC common + proxy compound from head-day scaling; waste = direct ESPAC per-kg waste feed when available, otherwise ESPAC waste from head-day scaling
Infra_water_electricity_note: ESPAC livestock modules do not expose direct infrastructure/water/electricity variables for this row; literature/system proxy attached
Infra_water_electricity_citations: FAO Rural structures in the tropics - Table 10.15 reports broiler drinking-water requirements around 0.20 to 0.35 L/bird/day at 9 to 12 weeks; the midpoint 0.275 L/bird/day was adopted. (https://www.fao.org/4/s1250e/S1250E0q.htm) | Iowa layer-house electricity summary - In the absence of a stronger broiler electricity factor in ESPAC, a conservative small-poultry-house electricity proxy of 0.005 kWh/bird/day was retained. (https://blogs.millersville.edu/aest/2022/05/13/dr-haughery-publishes-research-findings/) | FAO Good practices for the small-scale production of broiler chickens - Reports that under tropical conditions about 900 cm2 per broiler is sufficient for a 1.65 kg bird in the deep-litter system, described there as the most widely used broiler housing system; this was retained as a dominant broiler-house area proxy for Ecuador. (https://www.fao.org/4/y4991e/y4991e00.pdf)

Synthetic_headcount_basis_note: Milk headcount per kg is based on lifetime FPCM per cow using 6.6 lactations of 305 days each.

(https://www.fao.org/4/t1265e/t1275e01.htm) | FAO Milking, milk collection and transport - Gives wash-water benchmarks for hand, bucket, and pipeline milking plus plate-cooler water loads, supporting a dedicated dairy handling infrastructure proxy. (https://www.fao.org/4/i2433e/i2433e07.pdf) | Uruguay MGAP dairy intermediate-consumption report - Provides Latin American dairy infrastructure energy benchmarks for milking-room electricity and milk cooling; a rounded 1.5 kWh/cow/day proxy was adopted as a moderate regional milking-plus-cooling estimate. Infrastructure_type: Ecuador smallholder pigsty or swine pen with feeder and drinker
Synthetic_headcount_basis_note: Headcount per kg is based on current inventory mass basis for live weight equivalent products.
Synthetic_feed_mapping_note: pasture total = ESPAC pasture + proxy forage + proxy tree forage; cultivated and natural pasture totals show only the ESPAC-linked split of cattle pasture, while unmatched pasture is the residual cattle pasture share not linked to pcnac or sunac; supplement = direct ESPAC per-kg supplement/proxy-mineral feed when available, otherwise ESPAC supplement + proxy mineral from head-day scaling; common = direct ESPAC per-kg common/proxy-compound feed when available, otherwise ESPAC common + proxy compound from head-day scaling; waste = direct ESPAC per-kg waste feed when available, otherwise ESPAC waste from head-day scaling
Infra_water_electricity_note: ESPAC livestock modules do not expose direct infrastructure/water/electricity variables for this row; literature/system proxy attached
Infra_water_electricity_citations: FAO Livestock housing for pigs - Pig water consumption varies by class from about 2.5 L/day at 25 kg to 7.0 L/day at 90 to 120 kg; 7.0 L/head/day was adopted as a practical market-pig proxy for mixed ESPAC swine rows. (https://www.fao.org/3/s1250e/S1250E14.htm) | Caracterización de granjas porcinas de Banasur, Pasaje, El Oro - Provides Ecuador smallholder swine-system context and supports the use of a simple pen-based infrastructure proxy, although it does not provide a clean biological drinking-water factor. (https://polodelconocimiento.com/ojs/index.php/es/article/view/1043/html) | National Hog Farmer study summary - Finishing-barn electricity around 11.2 kWh per finished pig produced was annualized over a 180-day finishing period to derive a daily proxy. (https://www.nationalhogfarmer.com/livestock-management/study-goes-to-pig-barns-to-find-actual-energy-use)
